description:
To properly set the group owner of /etc/iptables, run the command: 
$ sudo chgrp root /etc/iptables
rationale:
The ownership of the /etc/iptables directory by the root group is important
because this directory hosts iptables configuration. Protection of this
file is critical for system security. Assigning the ownership to root
ensures exclusive control of the iptables configuration.
ocil_clause:
/etc/iptables does not have a group owner of
root
ocil:
To check the group ownership of /etc/iptables, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /etc/iptables
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following group-owner: root
fixtext:
Change the group of the file /etc/iptables to root by running the following command:
$ sudo chgrp root /etc/iptables
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/iptables file must be group-owned by root.
title: Verify Group Who Owns /etc/iptables Directory
